preamble.summary: Reuben lies with Bilhah, his father's concubine — a grave transgression. Jacob discovers it and is grieved but does not approach Bilhah again. The chapter transitions into extended halakhic material on sexual prohibitions, declaring that uncovering a father's bed is punishable by death.
preamble.remarkable: Jubilees uses the Reuben-Bilhah incident (Genesis 35:22) — reported in a single verse in the Torah — as the occasion for an elaborate legal code on sexual crimes.
preamble.friction: The severity of the penalty (death, no atonement) exceeds Leviticus 20:11, which prescribes death but does not deny atonement.
preamble.connections: Genesis 35:22 (Reuben and Bilhah); Leviticus 18:8; 20:11; Genesis 49:3-4 (Reuben's loss of birthright); Deuteronomy 27:20.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob went and dwelt to the south of the tower of Eder.
verses.rendering: Jacob went and settled south of the Tower of Eder. In the first year of the first week of the forty-fifth jubilee, he went up to Bethel.

verses.text_reference: And on the new moon of the first month he set out and went to the land of Canaan.
verses.rendering: At the new moon of the first month, he set out for the land of Canaan. He built an altar on the mountain and offered sacrifices.

verses.text_reference: And Reuben saw Bilhah, Rachel's maid, bathing in water in a secret place, and he desired her.
verses.rendering: Reuben saw Bilhah, Rachel's maid and his father's concubine, bathing in a secluded place. He desired her.
translator_notes: Jubilees adds the detail of Bilhah bathing — absent from Genesis.

verses.text_reference: And he hid himself at night, and entered the house of Bilhah, and found her sleeping alone.
verses.rendering: He concealed himself at night and entered Bilhah's house. He found her sleeping alone in her bed.

verses.text_reference: And he lay with her, and she awoke and saw Reuben, and cried out.
verses.rendering: He lay with her. She awoke and saw that Reuben was in bed with her. She cried out.
translator_notes: Bilhah's resistance establishes that this was assault, not a consensual act.

verses.text_reference: And she was ashamed and released her hand from him, and he fled.
verses.rendering: She was overcome with shame, and she released her grip on him. He fled.

verses.text_reference: And she lamented because of this thing exceedingly.
verses.rendering: She grieved bitterly over what had happened but told no one.

verses.text_reference: And when Jacob came, she said: 'I am not clean for thee, for Reuben has defiled me.'
verses.rendering: When Jacob came, she said to him: I am no longer clean for you. Reuben violated me in the night while I slept.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob was exceedingly wroth with Reuben because he had uncovered his father's skirt.
verses.rendering: Jacob was furious with Reuben for lying with Bilhah — for uncovering his father's bed.
translator_notes: 'Uncovering his father's bed' uses the same idiom as Leviticus 18:8.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob did not approach her again because Reuben had defiled her.
verses.rendering: Jacob never approached Bilhah again, because Reuben had defiled her. As for any man who uncovers his father's bed — his act is abominable before the Lord.

verses.text_reference: For this reason it is written on the heavenly tablets that a man should not lie with his father's wife.
verses.rendering: For this reason it is written and ordained on the heavenly tablets: a man must not lie with his father's wife and must not uncover his father's bed. They must both be put to death.

verses.text_reference: And there shall be nothing unclean before our God in the nation which He hath chosen.
verses.rendering: There must be no impurity before our God among the nation he has chosen for himself as his own possession.

verses.text_reference: And again, it is written: 'Cursed be he who lieth with the wife of his father.' And all the holy ones said 'So be it.'
verses.rendering: And again it is written: Cursed be the one who lies with his father's wife. And all the holy ones of the Lord said: So be it! So be it!
translator_notes: The 'holy ones' ratifying the curse echoes Deuteronomy 27:20.

verses.text_reference: And do thou, Moses, command the children of Israel that they observe this word; for it entaileth a punishment of death.
verses.rendering: And you, Moses, command the children of Israel to observe this law, for it carries the death penalty. There is no atonement — ever — for the man who does this.

verses.text_reference: For the day on which he did this he is to be slain.
verses.rendering: On the very day he commits this act, he must be killed. No sin offering, no guilt offering, and no burnt offering can atone for it.

verses.text_reference: And it is written that he shall be destroyed and cut off.
verses.rendering: It is written in the Torah of Moses that such a man must be destroyed and cut off, for he has committed a shameful crime in Israel.

verses.text_reference: And Reuben repented, and sought his father's forgiveness.
verses.rendering: Reuben repented and sought forgiveness from his father and his brothers.

verses.text_reference: And Jacob said: 'Let it be forgiven, for Reuben asked for forgiveness.'
verses.rendering: Jacob said: Let it be forgiven, since Reuben has asked for mercy, and it was a source of grief to him.
translator_notes: Jacob's forgiveness stands in tension with the 'no atonement' law just stated.

verses.text_reference: And for this reason it was ordained on the heavenly tablets, that the firstborn should not take the birthright.
verses.rendering: For this reason it was ordained and written on the heavenly tablets that the firstborn should not receive the birthright portion. It was to be given to the one next after him.
translator_notes: Reuben's loss of the birthright is here made a permanent heavenly decree.

verses.text_reference: And he repented and was forgiven, and he did not do this thing again.
verses.rendering: He repented and was forgiven, and he did not commit this act again.

verses.text_reference: And after this Jacob went south to Ephrath, that is, Bethlehem.
verses.rendering: After this, Jacob journeyed south to Ephrath — that is, Bethlehem — in the land of Canaan.

verses.text_reference: And Isaac was still alive.
verses.rendering: Isaac, Jacob's father, was still alive. Isaac blessed the God of his father Abraham.

verses.text_reference: And Isaac knew that the Lord's blessing had not departed from him all the days of his life.
verses.rendering: Isaac knew that the Lord's blessing had not departed from him all the days of his life. He gave thanks to the Lord God of Israel, the Holy One.
